name: lifelines (Survival Analysis for Python)

performance.notes: NumPy/SciPy-based; fast for typical clinical trial sizes

agent_readiness.notes: Survival analysis library with scikit-learn-style API. from lifelines import KaplanMeierFitter, CoxPHFitter. kmf = KaplanMeierFitter().fit(durations, event_observed=events). kmf.plot_survival_function(). kmf.median_survival_time_. Log-rank test: from lifelines.statistics import logrank_test; result = logrank_test(T1, T2, E1, E2). Cox PH: cph = CoxPHFitter().fit(df, duration_col='T', event_col='E'). cph.print_summary(). cph.hazard_ratios_. Weibull: WeibullAFTFitter. Time-varying covariates. Concordance index.

gotchas: Cox PH assumes proportional hazards — must validate with Schoenfeld residuals
Left-truncation support is limited compared to R's survival package
Not integrated with scikit-learn pipelines directly
best_when: Kaplan-Meier survival curves and log-rank tests
Cox Proportional Hazards regression for clinical/churn data
Customer churn analysis and time-to-event modeling
Medical/clinical trial survival analysis in Python
avoid_when: Competing risks (use scikit-survival or R cmprsk)
When R's survival package compatibility is needed
summary: lifelines provides survival analysis for Python. KaplanMeierFitter().fit(T, E).plot_survival_function(). logrank_test(T1, T2, E1, E2). CoxPHFitter().fit(df).print_summary(). hazard_ratios_. WeibullAFTFitter. Concordance index. MIT by Cameron Davidson-Pilon. Excellent documentation. Best for KM curves, Cox PH regression, and churn analysis. Proportional hazards assumption must be validated.
